chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
MEDICAL RETRIEVAL-AUGMENTED
GENERATION SYSTEM FOR
HEALTHCARE KNOWLEDGE ACCESS

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
INTRODUCTION
The exponential growth of medical data has made it
challenging to access relevant and reliable healthcare
information efficiently.
Traditional search systems lack contextual
understanding, while standalone AI models may
generate unverified or inaccurate responses.
To address this, we propose a Medical Retrieval-
Augmented Generation (RAG) system that combines
semantic search with domain-specific AI to deliver
accurate, evidence-based medical insights.
Traditional search systems like keyword search often
return large volumes of irrelevant information, which
makes it difficult for doctors to find precise answers
quickly.
Modern Large Language Models (LLMs) can generate
natural language responses but sometimes produce
hallucinations (incorrect information) because they rely
only

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
rge Language Models (LLMs) can generate
natural language responses but sometimes produce
hallucinations (incorrect information) because they rely
only on their training data.
To solve this issue, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
is used. RAG combines information retrieval and generative
AI to produce accurate, evidence-based medical answers.

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
PROBLEM STATEMENT
 Information Overload
Fragmented Data
Sources
The healthcare domain is overwhelmed with massive volumes of medical research, clinical data, and
rapidly evolving knowledge, making it increasingly difficult to quickly access relevant, reliable, and up-
to-date information.
Traditional search engines return unstructured and time-consuming results, requiring significant effort
to filter and interpret useful insights. At the same time, generative AI models often produce
hallucinated or non-verified medical responses, which can be risky in critical healthcare scenarios.
This creates a significant gap where users lack an intelligent system that can efficiently retrieve trusted
medical knowledge and generate accurate, context-aware, and evidence-based insights in real

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
n intelligent system that can efficiently retrieve trusted
medical knowledge and generate accurate, context-aware, and evidence-based insights in real time,
ensuring both reliability and usability.

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
PROPOSED SOLUTION
We propose MedIntel RAG, an advanced Medical Retrieval-
Augmented Generation System designed to provide accurate,
evidence-based medical knowledge retrieval and explanation.
The system integrates biomedical language models with
vector-based semantic search to ensure that every generated
response is grounded in real medical literature.
Key Capabilities
• Retrieve relevant medical research papers using semantic search
 • Generate context-aware explanations using BioMistral 7B
 • Reduce AI hallucinations by grounding responses in verified medical knowledge
 • Provide fast and intelligent access to biomedical information
How the System Works
User Query → Semantic Search → Medical Knowledge Retrieval → AI-Powered Explanation
Key Innovation
Unlike traditional AI chatbots, our

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
he System Works
User Query → Semantic Search → Medical Knowledge Retrieval → AI-Powered Explanation
Key Innovation
Unlike traditional AI chatbots, our system first retrieves
 relevant medical evidence and then generates answers based on that evidence,
 ensuring higher reliability and transparency.

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
Impacts Benefits of
Medical RAG Systems
1. Accurate Medical Information
Answers are generated using verified medical sources.
2. Reduced AI Hallucinations
The model relies on retrieved documents rather than only training
data.
3. Faster Knowledge Access
Doctors can quickly find medical information during clinical practice.
4. Evidence-Based Medicine
Responses are grounded in scientific research.
5. Support for Medical Education
Medical students can use the system for learning and research.

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
Future Scope
The system can be extended into a clinical decision support tool to assist doctors with real-time, evidence-
based recommendations.
• Integration with real-time hospital data and electronic health records (EHRs) can enable personalized
medical insights.
• Expansion to include more trusted datasets such as World Health Organization guidelines and global
clinical research for broader coverage.Development of a multilingual interface to make medical knowledge
accessible to users in regional languages.
• Addition of voice-based interaction to support hands-free usage for healthcare professionals and
patients.
• Incorporation of explainable AI features, showing sources and reasoning behind each generated response.
• Scaling the system into a mobile or web application for widespread

FINAL YEAR / MAJOR PROJECT

Enterprise Document Intelligence System

An Agentic RAG Platform for Automated Policy, Contract, and Report Analysis

Implementation Plan & Technical Blueprint

Document Version 1.0

Project Title

Enterprise Document Intelligence System

Category

Generative AI / Applied NLP / Agentic Systems

Core Stack

RAG + LLM Agent + Prompt Engineering Pipeline

Build Platform

Antigravity (Agentic Dev Environment)

Prepared For

Academic Submission / Final Year Project Evaluation

Status

Approved for Implementation

Table of Contents

1. Executive Summary

Modern organizations accumulate thousands of unstructured PDF documents — policies, contracts, compliance reports, and financial statements — that are searched manually, read line by line, and summarized by hand. This

ents — policies, contracts, compliance reports, and financial statements — that are searched manually, read line by line, and summarized by hand. This is slow, inconsistent, and error-prone, especially for legal and compliance-sensitive material.

The Enterprise Document Intelligence System (EDIS) is a full-stack AI platform that lets an organization upload its documents once and then interact with them intelligently: ask natural-language questions and receive grounded, cited answers; generate accurate summaries on demand; and deploy an autonomous “Document Analyst” agent that can plan and execute multi-step analysis tasks (e.g. “compare clause 4.2 across all vendor contracts and flag any with auto-renewal”).

The system is architected around four pillars that map directly to the four

clause 4.2 across all vendor contracts and flag any with auto-renewal”).

The system is architected around four pillars that map directly to the four project requirements: a secure multi-format Upload & Ingestion pipeline; a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) layer for grounded question answering; a Prompt Engineering layer dedicated to high-fidelity summarization; and an Agent layer that orchestrates tools autonomously to act as a virtual document analyst. A bonus module adds scheduled, automatic executive-summary generation.

Why this stands out in class

Most student RAG projects stop at “upload PDF → chat with PDF.” This project goes further: it separates RAG (retrieval) from Prompt Engineering (summarization) from Agentic reasoning (multi-step analysis) as three distinct, demonstrable layers — which is exactly how production enterprise AI systems are actually designed.

Built and orchestrated using Antigravity, giving you a visible, reproducible build log and agent-driven development workflow you can show during evaluation.

2. Problem Statement

Large organizations across legal, HR, finance, and compliance functions store critical knowledge inside thousands of disparate PDF files. Three concrete pain points motivate this project:

Information is locked in unstructured text. Contracts, policies, and reports are written in prose, not

ee concrete pain points motivate this project:

Information is locked in unstructured text. Contracts, policies, and reports are written in prose, not structured databases, so standard search (Ctrl+F, keyword search) fails to answer real questions like “which contracts expire in Q1 and have a penalty clause?”

Manual review does not scale. A compliance officer cannot read 4,000 policy documents to verify a new regulation is reflected everywhere it should be. Human review is slow, expensive, and inconsistent between reviewers.

Summarization quality varies by person and by day. Executive summaries written manually differ in structure, tone, and completeness depending on who wrote them and how much time they had.

EDIS addresses all three by combining retrieval (find the right passages),

, and completeness depending on who wrote them and how much time they had.

EDIS addresses all three by combining retrieval (find the right passages), generation (answer and summarize precisely), and agentic reasoning (chain multiple retrieval and generation steps together to complete a complex analytical task) into one coherent system.

3. Project Objectives

Build a secure ingestion pipeline that accepts policies, contracts, and reports in PDF (and optionally DOCX) format, validates them, and prepares them for retrieval.

Implement a Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline that answers natural-language questions about uploaded documents with source-grounded, citation-backed answers and minimal hallucination.

Apply structured prompt engineering techniques to produce consistent, high-quality document summaries at multiple levels of granularity (one-line, paragraph, and section-by-section).

Design and implement an autonomous Document Analyst agent capable of multi-step reasoning — planning a sequence of retrieval, comparison, and summarization actions to satisfy complex, compound user requests.

(Bonus) Automatically generate and schedule executive summaries for newly uploaded or updated documents without user intervention.

Demonstrate the system end-to-end with a working demo, a reproducible Antigravity

for newly uploaded or updated documents without user intervention.

Demonstrate the system end-to-end with a working demo, a reproducible Antigravity build log, and complete technical documentation (this report).

4. Scope

4.1 In Scope

Upload and process PDF documents across three categories: Policies, Contracts, Reports.

Text extraction, chunking, embedding, and vector storage for semantic search.

Conversational question answering grounded in retrieved document chunks (RAG).

Prompt-engineered summarization (whole-document and section-level).

An LLM-driven agent with tool access (retrieval tool, summarization tool, comparison tool, metadata tool).

A web-based interface for upload, chat, and viewing generated summaries/reports.

Automatic executive summary generation as a

n tool, metadata tool).

A web-based interface for upload, chat, and viewing generated summaries/reports.

Automatic executive summary generation as a background/bonus job.

4.2 Out of Scope (for this iteration)

OCR for scanned/handwritten documents (can be listed as future work).

Multi-tenant enterprise authentication/SSO (a simple login is sufficient for a class demo).

Fine-tuning a custom LLM — the project uses prompt engineering and RAG on top of an existing foundation model, not model training.

Real-time collaborative multi-user editing of summaries.

5. System Architecture

EDIS follows a layered architecture. Each layer corresponds to one of the four required features, which makes the system easy to explain, demo, and grade section by section.

5.1 High-Level Architecture Diagram

[See Figure 1 — Architecture Diagram, provided as a separate image file: architecture-diagram.png]

5.2 Layer-by-Layer Breakdown

Layer 1 — Ingestion & Upload

Accepts PDF (primary) and DOCX uploads for three document classes: Policies, Contracts, Reports.

Validates file type/size, extracts raw text and basic metadata (title, page count, upload date, document class).

Splits documents into overlapping chunks (recommended: 800–1000 tokens with ~150 token overlap) to preserve context across boundaries.

Generates vector embeddings for each chunk and stores them in a vector database

okens with ~150 token overlap) to preserve context across boundaries.

Generates vector embeddings for each chunk and stores them in a vector database alongside metadata (doc ID, category, page number).

Layer 2 — RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)

On a user question, embeds the query and performs a similarity search (top-k, e.g. k=5–8) against the vector store, optionally filtered by document category or specific document.

Re-ranks retrieved chunks (optional but recommended for higher precision) and assembles a context window.

Sends the question + retrieved context to the LLM with an instruction to answer only from the provided context and to cite the source document and page.

Returns the answer with inline citations, so every claim can be traced back to a specific document and page

and to cite the source document and page.

Returns the answer with inline citations, so every claim can be traced back to a specific document and page — critical for legal/compliance trust.

Layer 3 — Prompt Engineering (Summarization)

Uses a library of structured, reusable prompt templates rather than ad-hoc prompting — one template per summary type (one-line, executive, section-by-section, risk-flagging).

Applies techniques such as role-setting (“you are a senior contracts analyst”), explicit output schemas (JSON or fixed Markdown structure), few-shot examples for consistent tone, and chain-of-thought scratch reasoning that is discarded before the final answer is shown.

Summaries are generated per-document and cached so they don’t need to be regenerated on every view.

Layer 4 — Agent (Document Analyst)

An LLM agent with access to a defined toolset: search_documents, summarize_document, compare_documents, extract_clauses, get_metadata.

Given a compound instruction (e.g. “Find all contracts with an auto-renewal clause and summarize the risk for each”), the agent plans a sequence of tool calls, executes them, and synthesizes a final structured answer.

The agent uses a reasoning loop (think → act → observe → repeat) and stops when it has enough information to answer, or asks a clarifying question if the request is ambiguous.

Bonus Layer — Automatic Executive Summary Generation

Triggered automatically whenever a new document is uploaded or an existing

equest is ambiguous.

Bonus Layer — Automatic Executive Summary Generation

Triggered automatically whenever a new document is uploaded or an existing one is updated.

Runs the summarization prompt chain in the background and stores the result, optionally notifying the user once it is ready.

6. Technology Stack

The stack below is a recommended, proven combination. Substitute any row with an equivalent tool your instructor prefers (e.g. OpenAI instead of Claude, FAISS instead of Chroma) — the architecture does not change.

Layer

Recommended Technology

Why

Frontend / UI

React (Next.js) + Tailwind CSS

Fast to build, component-based, clean chat + dashboard UI

Backend API

Python — FastAPI

Async-friendly, ideal for AI/ML pipelines and streaming responses

LLM Provider

Claude

sed, clean chat + dashboard UI

Backend API

Python — FastAPI

Async-friendly, ideal for AI/ML pipelines and streaming responses

LLM Provider

Claude (Anthropic API)

Strong reasoning, native tool-use/agent support, large context window

Embeddings

Voyage AI / OpenAI text-embedding / Sentence-Transformers (local)

High-quality semantic embeddings for retrieval

Vector Database

ChromaDB (local/dev) or Pinecone / Qdrant (cloud)

Chroma is free and ideal for a class project; Pinecone/Qdrant for scale

Document Parsing

PyMuPDF (fitz) / pdfplumber

Reliable text + layout extraction from PDFs

Orchestration

LangChain or LlamaIndex (or custom lightweight pipeline)

Pre-built RAG and agent abstractions; speeds up development

Database (metadata)

PostgreSQL / SQLite

Stores document metadata, summaries, chat history

Dev / Build Environment

Antigravity (agentic IDE)

Agent-assisted, reproducible build process — unique differentiator

Deployment (optional)

Docker + Render / Railway / AWS

Containerized, portable demo deployment

7. Feature Deep Dive

7.1 Upload Module

Supports three document categories with category-specific metadata fields:

Category

Typical Metadata Captured

Special Handling

Policies

Policy ID, department, effective date, version

Detect superseded versions; flag policy conflicts

Contracts

Parties, effective/expiry date, contract type

Extract key clauses (termination, renewal, penalty)

Reports

Report type, reporting

flicts

Contracts

Parties, effective/expiry date, contract type

Extract key clauses (termination, renewal, penalty)

Reports

Report type, reporting period, author/department

Extract KPIs/figures into structured tables where possible

7.2 RAG Question Answering — Worked Example

Example user query: “What is the notice period for terminating the vendor agreement with Company X?”

Query is embedded and matched against vector store, filtered to category = Contracts and document = “Company X Agreement.”

Top 5 matching chunks are retrieved (e.g. clauses 7.1–7.4 covering termination).

Chunks + query are passed to the LLM with a strict instruction: “Answer using only the context below. If the answer is not present, say so. Cite the page number for every claim.”

LLM returns: “The notice

truction: “Answer using only the context below. If the answer is not present, say so. Cite the page number for every claim.”

LLM returns: “The notice period is 60 days (Section 7.2, p. 4).” — a precise, traceable answer instead of a generic summary.

7.3 Prompt Engineering — Summarization Template Example

Rather than a single generic “summarize this” prompt, EDIS uses a structured template, e.g.:

Role: You are a senior compliance analyst.
Task: Summarize the attached [document_type] in the following structure:
1. One-line purpose
2. Key obligations / parties
3. Critical dates and durations
4. Risk flags (if any)
Constraint: Do not infer facts not present in the document. Output as Markdown.

This structured, role-based, schema-constrained prompting produces consistent output regardless of which document is fed in — the core skill the “Prompt Engineering” feature is meant to demonstrate.

7.4 Agent — Document Analyst Reasoning Loop

Example compound instruction: “Which of our contracts auto-renew, and which one carries the highest financial penalty if we exit early?”

Agent plans: (a) search all contracts for auto-renewal clauses, (b) for each match, extract the early-exit penalty, (c) compare penalties, (d) produce a ranked answer.

Agent calls search_documents(category="contracts", query="auto-renewal clause").

For each returned document, agent calls extract_clauses(doc_id, clause_type="early termination penalty").

Agent compares extracted values internally, then calls a

rned document, agent calls extract_clauses(doc_id, clause_type="early termination penalty").

Agent compares extracted values internally, then calls a final synthesis step to produce a ranked, cited answer.

This loop — plan, act, observe, repeat, synthesize — is what distinguishes an agent from a simple chatbot, and is the centerpiece of the project’s technical novelty.

8. Data Model (Simplified Schema)

message_id, session_id, role, content, cited_chunks,

utive/section), content, generated_at

session_id, user_id, created_at

9. Implementation Roadmap

A 6-phase build plan designed to be executed inside Antigravity, where each phase can be delegated as a discrete agent task with clear headings/outputs — ideal for showing a clean, reproducible build log during evaluation.

Phase

Deliverable

Key Tasks

Phase 1

Setup & Ingestion

Working upload pipeline

Project scaffold; PDF/DOCX parser; chunking; embedding; vector DB setup

Phase 2

RAG Core

Working Q&A on one document

Retrieval pipeline; prompt template for grounded QA; citation formatting

Phase 3

Prompt Engineering

Reliable summarization

Template library (one-line / executive / section); output schema validation

Phase 4

Agent Layer

Multi-step analyst agent

Tool definitions; reasoning loop; multi-doc comparison logic

Phase 5

UI & Integration

End-to-end demo app

Upload UI; chat UI; summary viewer; agent task viewer

Phase 6

Bonus + Polish

Auto-executive-summary + docs

Background job trigger; testing; this implementation report; demo script

10. Evaluation & Testing Strategy

10.1 RAG Quality Metrics

Retrieval precision: are the retrieved chunks actually relevant to the query? (manual spot-check on a test set of 20–30 Q&A pairs)

Faithfulness: does the generated answer only use facts

ed chunks actually relevant to the query? (manual spot-check on a test set of 20–30 Q&A pairs)

Faithfulness: does the generated answer only use facts present in the retrieved context (no hallucination)?

Citation accuracy: does the cited page/section actually contain the claimed information?

10.2 Summarization Quality

Structure compliance: does every summary follow the defined schema (sections present, correct format)?

Completeness vs. conciseness: does the summary capture all critical obligations/dates without padding?

10.3 Agent Reliability

Task success rate across a fixed set of 10–15 compound test instructions of increasing complexity.

Tool-call correctness: does the agent call the right tool with the right arguments at each step?

Graceful failure: does the agent ask for

omplexity.

Tool-call correctness: does the agent call the right tool with the right arguments at each step?

Graceful failure: does the agent ask for clarification instead of guessing when an instruction is ambiguous?

10.4 System-Level Testing

Upload validation (file type/size limits, corrupted file handling).

Load testing with a realistic document set (recommend 50–100 sample PDFs for the class demo corpus).

End-to-end demo script rehearsal covering all four features plus the bonus feature.

11. Risks & Mitigations

Risk

Impact

Mitigation

LLM hallucination in answers

High — erodes trust, especially for legal/compliance use

Strict “context-only” prompting; mandatory citations; faithfulness checks

Poor PDF text extraction (scanned/complex layouts)

Medium — missing or garbled chunks

Use robust parser (PyMuPDF); flag low-confidence extractions; OCR as future work

Chunking breaks context across clause boundaries

Medium — incomplete answers

Overlapping chunks; section-aware chunking using headings where possible

Agent gets stuck in a reasoning loop / calls wrong tool

Medium — incorrect or incomplete answers

Max iteration limit; explicit tool schemas; fallback to direct RAG answer

API cost/rate limits during demo

Low–Medium — demo disruption

Cache summaries; use a small, fixed demo corpus; have an offline fallback recording

Sensitive document content (contracts/policies)

Medium —

uption

Cache summaries; use a small, fixed demo corpus; have an offline fallback recording

Sensitive document content (contracts/policies)

Medium — privacy/confidentiality

Use synthetic/sample documents for the class demo, not real organizational data

12. Differentiation — Why This Stands Out

Most classmates building a “document chatbot” will submit a single RAG loop: upload → embed → chat. EDIS is differentiated on four concrete axes:

Layered architecture, not a single loop. RAG, prompt engineering, and agentic reasoning are implemented and demoed as three separable, individually testable layers — mirroring how real enterprise AI platforms (e.g. legal-tech and compliance-tech products) are actually built.

A genuine agent, not just a chatbot. The Document Analyst plans and executes

forms (e.g. legal-tech and compliance-tech products) are actually built.

A genuine agent, not just a chatbot. The Document Analyst plans and executes multi-step tool-calling tasks (compare across documents, extract clauses, rank by risk) — well beyond single-turn Q&A.

Domain specificity. Built explicitly around three real enterprise document types (policies, contracts, reports) with category-aware metadata and extraction logic, not a generic “any PDF” demo.

Built with Antigravity. The build process itself is agent-assisted and reproducible, giving you a unique, demonstrable development story for evaluation — not just the final app.

Automatic executive summaries (bonus). A background job that proactively keeps leadership-ready summaries up to date — a feature with clear, tangible business value beyond the core requirements.

13. Suggested Demo Script (For Class Presentation)

Upload: Show 3–5 sample documents being uploaded (one policy, one contract, one report) and processed live.

RAG Q&A: Ask 2–3 natural questions (“What is the termination notice period in the vendor contract?”) and show the cited, grounded answer.

Prompt-engineered summary: Generate an executive summary of one document live and show the consistent structured output.

Agent task: Issue one compound instruction (“Compare auto-renewal risk across all contracts”) and narrate the agent’s plan → tool calls → final answer.

Bonus: Upload a new document

instruction (“Compare auto-renewal risk across all contracts”) and narrate the agent’s plan → tool calls → final answer.

Bonus: Upload a new document and show the executive summary being generated automatically in the background without being asked.

Close: Show the architecture diagram and the Antigravity build log as evidence of a structured, reproducible engineering process.

14. Future Enhancements

OCR support for scanned and handwritten documents.

Multi-language document support.

Role-based access control and full enterprise SSO integration.

Fine-tuned or distilled smaller model for cost-efficient summarization at scale.

Active-learning feedback loop where user corrections improve retrieval ranking over time.

Integration with e-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM)

arning feedback loop where user corrections improve retrieval ranking over time.

Integration with e-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM) platforms.

15. Conclusion

The Enterprise Document Intelligence System turns an organization’s static PDF archive into an interactive, queryable knowledge base. By cleanly separating ingestion, retrieval-augmented question answering, prompt-engineered summarization, and agentic multi-step analysis into distinct, demonstrable layers — and by automating executive summaries as a bonus capability — the project meets every stated requirement while remaining clear enough to explain confidently in a viva or class presentation. Built and tracked using Antigravity, it also tells a strong story about engineering process, not just the final output, which is what will make it stand out among submissions in your class.

for newly uploaded or updated documents without user intervention.

Demonstrate the system end-to-end with a working demo, a reproducible Antigravity build log, and complete technical documentation (this report).

4. Scope

4.1 In Scope

Upload and process PDF documents across three categories: Policies, Contracts, Reports.

Text extraction, chunking, embedding, and vector storage for semantic search.

Conversational question answering grounded in retrieved document chunks (RAG).

Prompt-engineered summarization (whole-document and section-level).

An LLM-driven agent with tool access (retrieval tool, summarization tool, comparison tool, metadata tool).

A web-based interface for upload, chat, and viewing generated summaries/reports.

Automatic executive summary generation as a

Pre-built RAG and agent abstractions; speeds up development

Database (metadata)

PostgreSQL / SQLite

Stores document metadata, summaries, chat history

Dev / Build Environment

Antigravity (agentic IDE)

Agent-assisted, reproducible build process — unique differentiator

Deployment (optional)

Docker + Render / Railway / AWS

Containerized, portable demo deployment

7. Feature Deep Dive

7.1 Upload Module

Supports three document categories with category-specific metadata fields:

Category

Typical Metadata Captured

Special Handling

Policies

Policy ID, department, effective date, version

Detect superseded versions; flag policy conflicts

Contracts

Parties, effective/expiry date, contract type

Extract key clauses (termination, renewal, penalty)

Reports

Report type, reporting

GENERATION SYSTEM FOR

Enterprise Document Intelligence System

An Agentic RAG Platform for Automated Policy, Contract, and Report Analysis

Implementation Plan & Technical Blueprint

Document Version 1.0

Project Title

Enterprise Document Intelligence System

Category

Generative AI / Applied NLP / Agentic Systems

Core Stack

RAG + LLM Agent + Prompt Engineering Pipeline

Build Platform

Antigravity (Agentic Dev Environment)

Prepared For

Academic Submission / Final Year Project Evaluation

Status

Approved for Implementation

Table of Contents

1. Executive Summary

Modern organizations accumulate thousands of unstructured PDF documents — policies, contracts, compliance reports, and financial statements — that are searched manually, read line by line, and summarized by hand. This

citations.filename: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
citations.text:
he System Works
User Query → Semantic Search → Medical Knowledge Retrieval → AI-Powered Explanation
Key Innovation
Unlike traditional AI chatbots, our system first retrieves
 relevant medical evidence and then generates answers based on that evidence,
 ensuring higher reliability and transparency.

Summaries are generated per-document and cached so they don’t need to be regenerated on every view.

Layer 4 — Agent (Document Analyst)

An LLM agent with access to a defined toolset: search_documents, summarize_document, compare_documents, extract_clauses, get_metadata.

Given a compound instruction (e.g. “Find all contracts with an auto-renewal clause and summarize the risk for each”), the agent plans a sequence of tool calls, executes them, and synthesizes a final structured answer.

The agent uses a reasoning loop (think → act → observe → repeat) and stops when it has enough information to answer, or asks a clarifying question if the request is ambiguous.

Bonus Layer — Automatic Executive Summary Generation

Triggered automatically whenever a new document is uploaded or an existing

okens with ~150 token overlap) to preserve context across boundaries.

Generates vector embeddings for each chunk and stores them in a vector database alongside metadata (doc ID, category, page number).

Layer 2 — RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)

On a user question, embeds the query and performs a similarity search (top-k, e.g. k=5–8) against the vector store, optionally filtered by document category or specific document.

Re-ranks retrieved chunks (optional but recommended for higher precision) and assembles a context window.

Sends the question + retrieved context to the LLM with an instruction to answer only from the provided context and to cite the source document and page.

Returns the answer with inline citations, so every claim can be traced back to a specific document and page

ents — policies, contracts, compliance reports, and financial statements — that are searched manually, read line by line, and summarized by hand. This is slow, inconsistent, and error-prone, especially for legal and compliance-sensitive material.

The Enterprise Document Intelligence System (EDIS) is a full-stack AI platform that lets an organization upload its documents once and then interact with them intelligently: ask natural-language questions and receive grounded, cited answers; generate accurate summaries on demand; and deploy an autonomous “Document Analyst” agent that can plan and execute multi-step analysis tasks (e.g. “compare clause 4.2 across all vendor contracts and flag any with auto-renewal”).

The system is architected around four pillars that map directly to the four

agentTasks.finalAnswer:
### 📋 Autonomous Agent Document Analysis Report
**Analyzed Instruction**: *"Compare contracts"*
**Execution Mode**: Offline Sandbox / Mock Mode
**Analyzed Corpus**: 1 Documents.

#### Key Parameters Extracted

| Document Name | Document Category | Auto-Renewal Active? | Notice Deadline | Risk Exposure Level |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf** | REPORT | YES (Auto-extensions) | 60 Days | 🔴 High (Locked terms) |


### Analytical Synthesis & Recommendations
1. **Notice Alert**: Ensure written termination cancellations are submitted at least **60 days prior** for documents containing automatic renew properties.
2. **Review Advisory**: Check the liability thresholds of all contracts flagged above. We recommend negotiating a hard cap equivalent to 100% of standard fees paid.
3. **Continuous Tracking**: Add monitoring to prevent auto-renewal dates from slipping past.

*Analysis generated automatically by EDIS Document Analyst Agent.*

### 📋 Autonomous Agent Document Analysis Report
**Analyzed Instruction**: *"Compare all uploaded documents"*
**Execution Mode**: Offline Sandbox / Mock Mode
**Analyzed Corpus**: 2 Documents.

#### Key Parameters Extracted

| Document Name | Document Category | Auto-Renewal Active? | Notice Deadline | Risk Exposure Level |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf** | REPORT | YES (Auto-extensions) | 60 Days | 🔴 High (Locked terms) |
| **EDIS_Implementation_Plan.docx** | REPORT | NO (Direct expiry) | N/A | 🟢 Low (Standard) |


### Analytical Synthesis & Recommendations
1. **Notice Alert**: Ensure written termination cancellations are submitted at least **60 days prior** for documents containing automatic renew properties.
2. **Review Advisory**: Check the liability thresholds of all contracts flagged above. We recommend negotiating a hard cap equivalent to 100% of standard fees paid.
3. **Continuous Tracking**: Add monitoring to prevent auto-renewal dates from slipping past.

*Analysis generated automatically by EDIS Document Analyst Agent.*
